statement: Two blocks are connected by a light inextensible rope over a frictionless massless pulley. Block A (mass 8 kg) hangs vertically on the left; Block B (mass 5 kg) hangs vertically on the right. The system is released from rest. Find the acceleration of the system and the rope tension T. Use g = 9.81 m/s².
common_student_errors: Writing separate equations with different tension values for each side of the pulley
Incorrectly assuming both weights subtract: using (mA - mB)·g = (mA + mB)·a but getting the sign wrong
Not recognizing that both blocks have the same magnitude of acceleration since the rope is inextensible
